tasks.title: Create Hero Section Component in src/components/HeroSection.tsx
tasks.description: Create a React functional component HeroSection in src/components/HeroSection.tsx. It should include a clear headline (e.g., 'Learn IELTS with a Clear Daily Roadmap'), a short subtitle explaining IELTS Journey's value, a primary CTA button labeled 'Start Your IELTS Journey', a secondary CTA button labeled 'See How It Works', and a placeholder area for a product preview or dashboard mockup. Use semantic HTML, accessible button states, and Tailwind CSS for styling with a clean modern SaaS style. Ensure responsiveness and good spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroSection component renders headline, subtitle, two CTA buttons, and preview area
Buttons have accessible states and semantic HTML
Component is responsive and styled with Tailwind CSS

commands: pnpm test src/components/HeroSection.test.tsx || echo 'Manual UI check required'

outputPlan.description: React component for the landing page hero section

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides the landing page's first impression and main call to action

tasks.title: Create Problem Section Component in src/components/ProblemSection.tsx
tasks.description: Create a React functional component ProblemSection in src/components/ProblemSection.tsx. This section explains common IELTS learner problems in a simple, emotional tone. Use bullet points for problems such as 'They do not know where to start', 'They waste time searching many resources', and 'They feel overwhelmed'. Use semantic HTML with headings and lists, Tailwind CSS for styling, and ensure responsiveness and accessibility.
acceptanceCriteria: ProblemSection component renders all specified problems as bullet points
Text is clear, emotional, and simple
Component is responsive and accessible

commands: pnpm test src/components/ProblemSection.test.tsx || echo 'Manual UI check required'

outputPlan.description: React component for the IELTS learner problem explanation section

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Explains user pain points to build empathy and context

tasks.title: Create Feature Section Component in src/components/FeatureSection.tsx
tasks.description: Create a React functional component FeatureSection in src/components/FeatureSection.tsx. This section displays nine feature cards for IELTS Journey's main features: Personalized IELTS Roadmap, Daily Study Plan, Ready-Made Learning Content, Vocabulary and Grammar Practice, Reading and Listening Practice, Progress Tracking, AI Tutor Assistant, Saved Materials, and Browser Extension Support. Each card includes a title, short description, and an icon or badge placeholder. Use Tailwind CSS for a clean card layout, ensure responsiveness and accessibility.
acceptanceCriteria: FeatureSection renders nine feature cards with correct titles and descriptions

commands: pnpm test src/components/FeatureSection.test.tsx || echo 'Manual UI check required'

tasks.title: Add SEO Metadata and Accessibility Improvements in src/pages/LandingPage.tsx and src/components/Layout.tsx
tasks.description: Add SEO metadata including page title, meta description, Open Graph title and description, and keywords related to IELTS study plan, roadmap, daily learning, self-study, and learning app. Implement proper heading structure across all components. Ensure semantic HTML usage and ARIA attributes for accessibility. Modify src/pages/LandingPage.tsx and create or modify src/components/Layout.tsx if needed to include <head> tags and metadata. Use React Helmet or equivalent for metadata management.
acceptanceCriteria: Page includes correct SEO metadata tags
Heading structure is logical and semantic
Accessibility attributes are present and correct
Page passes basic accessibility checks

outputPlan.description: Add SEO metadata and accessibility improvements
Layout component managing page metadata and structure

evidence: SEO metadata tags present in page source
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Improves search engine visibility and accessibility compliance

tasks.title: Integrate Landing Page into Application Routing in src/App.tsx
tasks.description: Modify src/App.tsx to add a route for the landing page at the root path '/'. Import LandingPage component and configure React Router v7 to render it. Ensure the landing page is the default entry point. Maintain existing app structure and routing conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: LandingPage is accessible at '/' route
Routing changes do not break existing navigation
App compiles and runs without errors

commands: pnpm test src/App.test.tsx || echo 'Manual routing test required'

outputPlan.description: Add landing page route to application router

metadata.inputContext: LandingPage component created and SEO added
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Makes landing page accessible as the app entry point

tasks.title: Perform Responsive Design and Accessibility Testing for Landing Page
tasks.description: Perform manual and automated testing of the landing page across desktop, tablet, and mobile screen sizes. Verify responsive layout, spacing, typography, and visual hierarchy. Use accessibility testing tools (e.g., axe, Lighthouse) to check for semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, color contrast, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility. Document any issues found for future fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page displays correctly on all tested screen sizes
Accessibility tests report no critical issues
Keyboard navigation and screen reader usage verified

metadata.inputContext: Landing page fully implemented and integrated
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Ensures landing page is production-ready and accessible
